prompt: Please build this Figma screen as a new feature in our Angular app: https://www.figma.com/design/zxKV5PL63p3nDkbR62MR03/Material-3-You--Sample-App-with-great-Prototype--Community-?node-id=24-3285&t=SaRBq3tmWNC9JpTy-4

assertions.text: Output explicitly asks the developer for a user story / behavior description before generating code
Output shows evidence the agent attempted to fetch the frame via the Figma MCP server (mcp__figma__* tools) rather than guessing from the URL
Output contains a structured audit covering at minimum: token census (bound vs. raw counts), component identification, inconsistencies, and missing states
Output contains a numbered list of open questions for the developer to answer before coding
Output addresses (or at least names) each of the five risks: missing tokens, token gaps, inconsistencies, hierarchy, missing states
Output does NOT generate finalized Angular code that silently invents tokens, layouts, or behavior not present in the Figma file
Output proposes a feature folder name, route, and component breakdown that fits src/app/features/<feature>/{feature,ui,data,util} structure
Output explicitly addresses how tablet and desktop should adapt (either using a variant from Figma or asking the developer)
Output references --mat-sys-* tokens or mat.<component>-overrides for theming, not raw hex
Output mentions a validation plan: ng lint, ng build, playwright-cli visual checks across breakpoints
